You are good, very good.  Soon you will be a master with your sword.  However, the real battle is not always won by a swing or a thrust.  To fight with Honor is not always easy.  Sometimes the Way of Honor will seem foolish to those that have none.  Nevertheless, without Honor, there is no victory.  With Honor, there is no real defeat.
You need to defend better.  You hit pretty often, but you get hit too often.
Don't get into combat with Scorpion until you dodge better.
You getting much better, but you still be not defending enough.
Do not let me touch you with my spear the next time.  I will be plenty mad if some monster tears you to shreds because you do not dodge enough.
Enough for now.  You look pretty winded.  We go back to Hall.
You got a good swing.  My shield arm is sore.
That time, we both got workout.  Pretty soon, I be needing rest.
You be getting past my shield many times now.  You be getting good.
Brigands in the desert better watch out for you.  They be in big trouble.
The TerrorSaurus who tries to eat you will be dead meat first.
You can take on a pack of Jackalmen now.
The Ghoulyman who tries to get you now will be sorry.
That be enough for now.  Soon you be giving me lessons.
Either I be out of shape from Simba or you be getting too good for me plenty soon.
You very fast learner.  I think maybe you be plenty tough enough now.
That be enough for today.  Soon you be giving me lessons.
Block my spear or get out of the way.  If it be scorpion's tail, you be dead.
Dodge!  Don't take damage, get out of the way.
Move out of the way more.  You got feet, use them.
Don't rely on your weapon to save you. Use your body to avoid damage.
You let me get you with spear way too often.  If I be ghoul, you be plenty cold right now.
Never stand still.  Moving target harder to hit.
TerrorSaurus is stupid but strong.  Takes a while before it knows it is dead.
Watch my actions.  Move before I do.
Swing high, swing low.  Keep enemy guessing where your weapon will be.
Don't watch my weapon, watch my eyes.  Let them show you where I will hit and when.
Put your body into your thrusts.
Brigands don't defend well.  Keep up your attacks.
If I be Griffin, swing high.  If I be Ghoul, swing low.  Use best attack for monster.
Scorpion always wiggles tail before it strikes.  Be ready to dodge.
Jackalmen may be cowards, but they do fight well.  Keep sword on one at a time.  Take it out as fast as possible.
Keep your shield held higher.  Easier to block spears and swords with it there.
When fighting against a sword, do not let a swing catch you off guard.  You can be disarmed that way.
Do not let yourself be distracted by thoughts.  Let yourself be one with your weapon.
Let your motion flow smoothly as you dodge.  Always remain in balance.

You are now a very knowledgeable Wizard. However, on occasion, you find yourself wondering what ever happened to Abdulla Doo, the Kattas, and to all your other friends in Shapeir.  And you wonder: wouldn't it have been better to have endured your "Trial by Fire"?

the portrait of Harry Houdini, the Escape Artist.

As the pieces of pottery hurtle towards you, you realize that you can't possibly catch them all.  Several fall to the floor, crashing loudly and alerting the old man.  The pottery was worthless, so your sentence is less severe than it might have been.  (They cut only one of your hands off.)
The large rug is too large to steal.  It simply covers what's beneath it.
The heavy wooden door leads to the alley.
Drapes cover a doorway which undoubtedly leads to the old man's bed chamber.
As it strikes the drapes, the sound frightens the old man.  He calls for his sons, who are angry.  VERY angry.  And violent.  VERY, VERY violent.

Huh.  Must've been my imagination again.
Huh.  I'm hearing things again, I guess.
Huh.  Kamar, your father is becoming senile.

Use the Enter key or the left mouse button to choose a character type.
Fighters tend to be rough, tough, and rowdy.  Choose the Fighter if you want to take the direct approach to your problems.
Wizards are known for their powerful spells and subtle, indirect solutions to problems.
Thieves have a variety of special skills, such as picking locks, hiding, and climbing walls.  The thief tends to work around problems rather than attacking them directly.
